executor_handoff.system_instructions: Execute one path only. Preserve existing test patterns and assertions. Follow scope exactly — do NOT widen. Swift 6.3 strict concurrency. NEVER add `@unchecked Sendable` to fix a diagnostic. Do NOT touch: FindingsLog, IDBox, or the 5 existing lock-backed `@unchecked Sendable` sites (MetricsCapturingDelegate, ManualClock, OutputRecorder, ScriptedStreamFetcher, GatedFetcher) — all out of scope. Changes confined to test target `ValistreamTests`. No production (`ValistreamCore`) edits.

task_brief.objective: Make `ValistreamTests` target compile under Swift 6.3 strict concurrency. 17 errors, all `#SendingClosureRisksDataRace` / `#SendingRisksDataRace`. Two moves: (A) `LiveSessionHarness` class -> actor; (B) restructure 8 event-collector closures to return their array instead of mutating a captured `var`.
task_brief.why: Pure-SPM + Swift 6 strict-concurrency migration broke ValistreamTests. Root cause: non-Sendable `LiveSessionHarness` captured into `Task`/`addTask` sending closures, plus collectors mutating test-local `var`s across a task boundary. Skill-aligned fix: actor over `@unchecked`, structured return-value over shared-mutable capture. (`@unchecked` is NOT the cause — zero errors come from those sites.)
task_brief.repo_signals: - `LiveSessionHarness` (Tests/ValistreamTests/Support/LiveSessionHarness.swift): `final class`, owns `var runTask` (the only mutable state -> reason it's non-Sendable). Members: `let clock = ManualClock()`, `let fetcher: ScriptedStreamFetcher`, `let session: ValidationSession`, `private let epoch`. Methods: start/waitForSleepers/step/advance/abortAndFinish/finish. - `ValidationSession` = `public actor` (Sendable). `session.events` / `session.timestampedEvents` = nonisolated Sendable `AsyncStream`s (already accessed sync in tests). - `ManualClock`, `ScriptedStreamFetcher`, `OutputRecorder` already Sendable (lock-backed `@unchecked` + `OSAllocatedUnfairLock`) — sync-accessible, leave as-is. - Existing test idiom: build harness, stub/timeline fetcher, `harness.start()`, drive via `harness.step(...)`, collect events from the session streams, `harness.abortAndFinish()`, assert.
task_brief.scope:
ONLY the 17 listed diagnostics in `ValistreamTests`. Two buckets:
Bucket 1 — fixed by `class -> actor` alone (9 sites; add `await` to start()): LiveSessionHarness.swift:65 (start()'s own `Task { await session.run() }` captures self) HeartbeatMonotonicTests.swift:41, :87 (driver `addTask { await harness.step... }`) RosterAndZeroURLTests.swift:95 (driver `addTask { await harness.waitForSleepers... }`) VerboseDistinctnessTests.swift:94, :127 (driver `addTask { await harness.step... }`) LiveMonitoringTests.swift:75, :109 (`Task { ...timestampedEvents; return events }`) LiveFaultScenarioTests.swift:35 (`Task { ...timestampedEvents; return events }`)
Bucket 2 — collector mutates a captured `var` -> return-value restructure (8 sites): HeartbeatMonotonicTests.swift:34 (`totals`), :80 (`lastTotal`) RosterAndZeroURLTests.swift:90 (`collectedEvents`) VerboseDistinctnessTests.swift:89 (`events`), :122 (`events`) VerbosityEquivalenceTests.swift:53 (`machineEvents`), :62 (`renderer` var; `.render` mutates) OutputLocationStartupTests.swift:60 (`events`) — NOTE: no harness here; builds `ValidationSession` directly. Actor change irrelevant; only the return-value fix applies.
Out of scope: FindingsLog, IDBox, the 5 lock-backed `@unchecked` sites, any ValistreamCore edit.
task_brief.implementation_path:
Step 1 — `LiveSessionHarness`: change `final class` to `actor`. Body unchanged EXCEPT `start()` -> `func start() { runTask = Task { [session] in await session.run() } }` (explicit `[session]` capture avoids retaining the harness in the task). clock/fetcher/session are immutable Sendable `let`s = nonisolated -> stay sync-accessible from tests; `runTask` becomes actor-isolated (fine). init unchanged.
Step 2 — Add `await` before every `harness.start()` (all callers are already `async`).
Step 3 — For each Bucket-2 site, convert the collector that mutates an outer `var` into a child task that RETURNS its accumulation; read it after the drive completes:
let collector = Task { [harness] in var acc: [SessionEvent] = [] for await e in harness.session.events { acc.append(e) } // or .timestampedEvents return acc } // ...existing drive (now on the current task)... await harness.abortAndFinish() let events = await collector.value
Where the site used `withDiscardingTaskGroup { addTask(collector) + addTask(driver) }`, drop the group: keep the collector as the `Task` above, run the driver inline on the current task, then `await collector.value`. Preserve original collector-vs-start ordering.
Special cases: - VerbosityEquivalenceTests:62 — move `var renderer = makeRenderer(recorder:...)` INSIDE the render task (capture `recorder`, the Sendable `OutputRecorder`); results read from `recorder` afterward. The `machineTask` (:53) uses the standard return-value pattern. - OutputLocationStartupTests:60 — no harness. `session` (actor) + `eventsStream` local are Sendable; only convert the collecting `Task` to return its `[SessionEvent]`.
task_brief.code_level_guidance: - Sendability facts to rely on: ValidationSession = actor (Sendable); events/timestampedEvents = nonisolated Sendable AsyncStream; ManualClock/ScriptedStreamFetcher/OutputRecorder = Sendable. - The ONLY new `await` introduced is on `start()`. fetcher.stub/timeline, session.* (already awaited), step/advance/waitForSleepers/abortAndFinish are unaffected. - Sequencing: the collector loop exits only when the stream finishes, which happens after `abortAndFinish()`. So `await collector.value` MUST come after `abortAndFinish()` or it deadlocks against `.timeLimit`. - Behavioral parity: restructured collectors must gather the IDENTICAL event set as before (same stream, same filter). Assertions must not change. - Forbidden: any new `@unchecked Sendable`; any `nonisolated(unsafe)`; touching out-of-scope types.
task_brief.search_fallback: If line numbers drifted: grep within `sources/Valistream/Tests/ValistreamTests` for `withDiscardingTaskGroup`, `group.addTask`, `harness.start()`, `LiveSessionHarness`, and `var events` / `var totals` / `var machineEvents`. Re-run the build (see validation) to get the authoritative current diagnostic line set.
task_brief.acceptance_criteria: - `swift build --build-tests --package-path sources/Valistream` -> 0 errors (was 17). - All 17 listed diagnostics gone; no new warnings/errors introduced. - No new `@unchecked Sendable` / `nonisolated(unsafe)`. FindingsLog, IDBox, and the 5 lock-backed sites are byte-for-byte untouched. - Test assertions/semantics unchanged; touched suites still pass.
task_brief.validation: Build: swift build --build-tests --package-path sources/Valistream 2>&1 | xcsift -f toon (status: success) Test: swift test --package-path sources/Valistream 2>&1 | xcsift -f toon (all pass) Then validate the `Valistream-Package` scheme build + test green (project gate). Spot-check the 7 touched suites — esp. Heartbeat (monotonic totals), VerbosityEquivalence (machine vs human parity), LiveMonitoring (hold/staleness) — for behavioral parity.
